entry.title: Stratoware and Miltech Simulations Release MBB Bo 105 Trailer
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Stratoware and Miltech Simulations have recently released a trailer for their upcoming MBB Bo 105 helicopter for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024. First announced in January as part of a partnership with Miltech Simulations, the MBB Bo 105 is being developed for both Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, with a planned release window in Q1 2026. The Bo 105 is a twin-engine light utility helicopter, originally developed in West Germany, and is known for its rigid rotor system, enabling some impressive agility and aerobatic capability. According to the developers, the aircraft is being built with input from a real-world Bo 105 pilot, and the audio is being built using real recordings. Multiple variants are planned, including civil, military, and specialised configurations. During FSWeekend 2026, we had the opportunity to take a closer look at the aircraft, speaking with managing director Aditya Sethi. More coverage from that will be published soon. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Navigraph Releases Charts Approach Bundle
entry.summary: Sim: OTHER. Navigraph has released a new “Approach Bundle” for its Charts application, introducing three new tools designed to enhance approach planning. The update, which was first previewed during FSWeekend 2026 last Saturday, is now available in the latest version of Navigraph Charts. At the core of the update are three main features: Approach Feathers, Extended Runway Centerlines, and a new Measurement Tool, each designed to provide a more intuitive overview of approach and navigation data. Approach Feathers offer a graphical representation of approach procedures overlayed on the map, displaying the final approach path, alongside key information such as course, frequency, and identifier. This allows for quicker orientation during arrivals, particularly in higher workload situations, as charts can be referenced seamlessly without needing to open or switch between them individually. The Extended Runway Centerlines feature builds on this by projecting the runway centerline outward on the map, giving you a quick reference. Rounding out the update is the Measurement Tool, which enables the calculation of distance and heading between points directly on the chart. This can be used to check waypoint spacing, estimate shortcuts, or quickly assess alternate routing options, without needing external tools. Together, these features form what Navigraph describes as a more “visual and precise” approach to workflow, where the goal was clarity and ease of use. The update is available now to Navigraph subscribers through the Charts application. Get yourself a subscription here. You can also read more on Navigraph's Blog. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: FlightFactor Teases 777-300ER for X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. FlightFactor has offered a brief teaser on its long-awaited Boeing 777-300ER for X-Plane 12, releasing a set of new preview images on their social channels, alongside a short and witty “Soon™” message. This follows the continued development of the 777 v2 series for XP12, which began with the release of the 777-200ER in beta, and which has now recently expanded with the freighter. That same freighter also received recognition at the 2026 Flight Sim Awards, where it was awarded Best Aircraft for X-Plane. The newly shared images are shot from the exterior, showing off some of the wider artwork and textures. FlightFactor has not shared additional details or a more precise timeline, though the latest suggests that the 777-300ER for X-Plane 12 may not be too far off. View the full post here. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: TriTriSim Announces GFX Airliner Effects Addon for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. TriTriSim has announced GFX Airliner for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, a visual effects enhancement add-on designed to bring more atmosphere to your sim. The announcement includes an official trailer and development update, confirming a planned release date of April 23. TriTriSim says GFX Airliner will provide physics-based visual effects, aiming to simulate atmospheric interaction that is not currently represented in the default simulator. This includes features such as wing condensation, engine vortices, and dynamic spray effects during wet runway operations, reacting in real time to conditions such as humidity, pressure, and aircraft behaviour. The add-on is positioned similarly to Parallel 42’s SimFX and Immersion Packs for MSFS 2020. TriTriSim says GFX is driven by real-time CFD simulations, with the effects appearing only when atmospheric conditions such as humidity, temperature, and angle of attack allow for it, although this can be overridden for cinematic purposes. Planned additions also include contrails and wing fade interaction with clouds. GFX Airliner will be available at an introductory price of $14.99 USD, before increasing to $19.99 USD shortly after release. We will keep you updated on its release. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Aerosoft/ToLiSS Update A340-600 Pro for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Aerosoft/ToLiSS have recently released their first update for the newly released A340-600 Pro for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, bringing multiple fixes and improvements based on community feedback. Update 1.0.1, the first so far, improves the waypoint-skipping feature, tweaks in-flight wingflex, and adds drag and fuel-flow improvements at cruise altitude, among other changes. The complete changelog follows below: They also stated that they are already working on further updates, including a wing remodel and certain parts of the fuselage, to enhance the model’s accuracy and quality. Furthermore, they have suggested a community-made cockpit lighting mod if one does not want to wait for the official lighting update, which is currently under development. It’s available on Aerosoft’s store for roughly $80, requiring at least 17 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: SiamFlight Releases Incheon International Airport for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. SiamFlight has recently released an early access version of their Incheon International Airport (RKSI) rendition for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, the largest airport in South Korea and among the busiest in the world, with a yearly average of 70 million passengers! Incheon opened in 2001 to replace Gimpo International Airport as the main airport in South Korea and handle the extra international air traffic that the former airport was unable to handle (as seen during the 1988 Olympics and the early 90s). Construction work began in 1992 on reclaimed land between Yeongjong Island and Youngyu Island, taking 8 years to complete and facing a delay due to an economic crisis. It is the main hub for Korean Air, Asiana Airlines, Jeju Air, and Polar Air Cargo. The current record figures stand at around 71 million passengers a year, with Korean Air accounting for 26% of the market share, followed by Asiana Airlines and Jeju Air with 18.04% and 8.09%, respectively. Destination-wise, Hong Kong is the busiest, with 3,149,768 passengers, followed by Osaka-Kansai (2,840,003) and Tokyo-Narita (2,783,677). The early-access release features a fully modeled terminal 1 and 2 (with the latest expansion), all satellite concourses with accurate architecture, cargo aprons 1 and 2, custom ground textures, custom runway textures, custom jetways, hand-crafted ground markings, custom night lighting, and performance-friendly optimization on both platforms. Future updates will include more detail both externally and internally, a custom GSX Pro profile, custom VDGS for the Xbox/marketplace version, more points of interest, cargo 2 apron warehouse texture improvements, more ground clutter, and more. The developers claim it is a true community-driven project, created in close partnership with the dedicated Korean flight simulation community. Updates will be released for free based on community feedback to keep on improving the product. It’s available on SimMarket for roughly $26.65, requiring at least 3.49 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: X-Codr Designs Releases Airport Enhancement Package V2 for X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. Scenery developer, X-Codr designs has released a massive update to their existing Airport Enhancement Package for X-Plane 12, with the product now at version 2. In this update, the developer has completely overhauled existing assets, built from the ground up. With this update, X-Codr has included new hangar models, new vehicle models, new clutter objects, and improved ground textures. New to X-Plane, as well, the developer has included procedurally generated airport overlays. The addon will apply rules to default scenery, that then determines where to place objects such as skids on taxiways, grunge at gates, tiedowns, cones, fuel hydrants, etc. Dynamic snow has been included as well. Instead of snow simply appearing everywhere at an airport, it accumulates more heavily in areas with less traffic, and less snow exists on taxiway centrelines, aprons, etc, where traffic usually operates. Lastly, the developer has significantly improved taxiway signs at default airports with much more detailed and accurate models. The signs are based on published FAA data, with a close match in colouring, sizing, font, and character spacing, to enhance realism. Thankfully, this comes with practically zero performance impact. To view the original blog post, head over to X-Codr Design’s website. If you already own this addon, the developer has kindly made it a free update, even though significant work has been done. If you are not already an owner of the addon, it can be purchased from the X-Plane.org store for $22.95.

entry.title: X-Codr Designs Updates KDEN and KEVV for X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. X-Codr Designs has released new updates for both Denver International Airport (KDEN) and Evansville Regional Airport (KEVV) for X-Plane 12. With this update, the developer has brought improved snow textures to the sceneries, to go along with their recently updated Airport Enhancement Package (AEP). Although the sceneries already had snow textures, they now have varying amounts of snow depending on the location of the airport. For example, the centreline of taxiways will have less snow than other areas, due to more vehicle traffic. Furthermore, the update brings other additions, including reduced VRAM usage, support for sharing assets with AEP, some minor fixes, and SkunkCrafts Updater support. For existing customers, this is a free update, which can be updated using the SkunkCrafts Updater. For new customers, KDEN can be purchased for $29.95, and KEVV can be purchased for $21.95.

entry.title: SimWorksStudios Releases Kodiak 100 for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Having initially released for MSFS 2020 with wide praise, SimWorks Studios has now released the aircraft for MSFS 2024, built using the 2024 SDK, and bringing with it a plethora of new features. ‍ Firstly, the Kodiak 100 has seen a visual improvement, building upon the free visual upgrade from 2020. The aircraft now includes a fully modelled engine bay, cargo pod interior, and various modeling and texturing improvements. Furthermore, animated passengers have also now been simulated, and unique cargo types have been added for each cabin variant. Excitingly, the aircraft now features the walkaround mode featured in MSFS 2024, allowing users to open and remove covers, inspect the brakes, wheels, and check oil levels. Various other features have been included with this big update, and you can view the full feature list below: The developer has noted that the amphibian version of the Kodiak 100 is now bundled with the base package, however, the standalone option still remains. Furthermore, the aircraft now fully works with the MSFS 2024 career mode, which is restricted to the wheeled variant only due to restrictions within the simulator. SimWorks Studios has teased that there is more to come with the Kodiak 100, with the development team currently testing the integration of Navigraph charts in the G1000, and further pre-flight features. Lastly, they noted that further improvements are coming with Sim Update 5, and these features will not only improve the Kodiak 100, but will translate to their other products. ‍ To purchase the SimWorks Studios Kodiak 100, head over to the developer’s store, where the airplane can be bought for €34.99. Existing users of the MSFS 2020 version of the aircraft, can receive a 50% discount as well. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Carenado Releases the B200 King Air for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Long time aircraft developer Carenado, has released the Beechcraft Kingair 200 exclusively for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024. The B200 is a pressurized, twin engine, turboprop, powered by two PT-6 engines, delivering a range of 1500-1700 nautical miles, a service ceiling of 35,000 feet, and cruise speeds around 250 knots. Carenado’s rendition of the B200 comes with various features, including working circuit breakers, landing gear emergency extension, realistic sounds, realistic flight dynamics, and more. You can view the full feature list below. Feature List: The Carenado B200 can be purchased only from the MSFS 2024 marketplace, for a price of CAD 31.99.

entry.title: iniBuilds Releases TriStar Airliner for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has recently released their rendition of the Lockheed TriStar Airliner for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, one of the most iconic widebodies ever made, representing a unique era where classic analog charm met advanced automation. Conceived in the 1960s and first flown in the early 1970s, the TriStar was Lockheed’s attempt to enter the jet era with an aircraft ahead of everything at the time in many ways, being the first widebody jet to feature autoland, an extremely remarkable feat at the time. It also featured a very advanced “active control system” that would automatically counter turbulence to warrant a smoother ride, a direct lift control (DLC) system that would allow for very smooth descents by using spoilers to adjust lift rather than changing the plane’s pitch. It was powered by Rolls-Royce RB211 turbofan engines (extremely ahead of their time as well), and their sheer complexity and development costs nearly bankrupted Rolls-Royce and greatly delayed the airplane’s production. Due to engine delays and its very high price tag, the TriStar didn’t prove very popular in the airline market, selling only 250 units, which meant they didn’t turn a profit and ultimately decided to exit the commercial aviation market forever. The TriStar’s legacy of good safety records and overall reliability remained exemplary throughout its service life, and it was, contrary to most airliners, absolutely loved by its pilots and maintenance crew alike. The add-on features an accurate rendition of the airplane, with a detailed engine simulation, Direct Lift Control (DLC) simulation, complete hydraulic, pneumatic, and electrical systems with realistic redundancy and load management, realistic fuel system, integrated moving map and INS waypoint management, realistic INS behavior, realistic lighting environment, accurate PBR material system, and much more. The complete feature list follows below: Systems Visuals Sounds Art & Effects PFB It’s exclusively available in the MSFS Marketplace for $49.99. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: TechnoBrain Teases Upcoming Tokyo International Airport for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. TechnoBrain has recently teased the progress on their rendition of Tokyo International Airport (RJTT) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020/2024. The airport serves the Greater Tokyo Area and averages 78.7 million passengers per year, making it the busiest airport in Japan. The airport started its life in 1931 as a tiny airfield with a 300-meter-long concrete runway, a small passenger terminal, and two hangars. Traffic figures grew throughout the 1930s, leading to the extension of the runway to 800 meters and the construction of a new, also 800-meter-long, runway. The airport's land area also increased from 53 hectares to 72.8 hectares. ​ It was also during that time period that the Japanese government started planning to build a new Tokyo Municipal Airport on an artificial island in Koto Ward. At the time, it was five times the size of Haneda and easily the largest airport in the world by the era's standards. Work was scheduled to start in 1939 and finish by 1941, but World War 2 put a permanent end to it. Post World War 2, Haneda was occupied by the United States Army, becoming Haneda Army Air Base. Many nearby residents were evicted during that time so they could carry out further expansion to accommodate their infrastructure. Both runways were extended to 1,650 meters and 2,100 meters, respectively. By the time everything was finally completed in 1946, the airport was already occupying 257.4 hectares, slightly larger than the old Tokyo Municipal Airport project. Part of the base was returned to Japan in 1952 and named Tokyo International Airport. The remainder remained under American control until it was returned to Japan in 1958.​ When Japan Airlines began domestic operations from the airport, there was no passenger terminal. This became a reality in 1955 after two years of hard work, followed by an extension for international flights in 1963.​ ‍ ‍ The introduction of jet aircraft in the 1960s led to many changes and improvements, including runway extensions, the repurposing of runways as aprons, and the construction of a new international arrivals facility. In the late 1970s, Narita Airport was inaugurated, taking most of the international service in the area and relegating Haneda to a primarily domestic airport. ​ For the next 32 years, Haneda lived through its "domestic era," a period in which many expansion projects were carried out to ensure it could actually serve Greater Tokyo properly: a new landfill in the mid-1980s, a new passenger terminal, new runways in the late 1990s/early 2000s, and a new international terminal in 1998. After completing a third international terminal in October 2010, the government reopened the airport to overseas operations, provided they operated between 11 p.m. and 7 a.m. ​ The international terminal was further expanded in 2014 with a new eight-gate pier, an expanded apron, an expanded check-in, and more. ​ It's a hub for All Nippon Airways and Japan Airlines and an operating base for Air Do, Skymark Airlines, Solaseed Air, and StarFlyer. Air Canada, Air China, Air France, American Airlines, Asiana Airlines, British Airways, Cathay Pacific, China Airlines, Delta Air Lines, Emirates, EVA Air, Finnair, Garuda Indonesia, ITA Airways, Korean Air, Lufthansa, Qantas, Scandinavian Airlines, Singapore Airlines, Thai, Turkish Airlines, United Airlines, and Vietnam Airlines also serve it. The developer has stated it’s scheduled for release this summer, promising a high level of quality and attention to detail, urging their fans to look forward to it. ​If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: BlueBird Shares New 757 EFB Preview
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. BlueBird Simulations has shared a brief glimpse of the Boeing 757-200’s electronic flight bag, showcasing a few of the menus and workflow pages. The preview was posted on their official Discord Server. The images show the EFB open with various tabs and pages, including Cockpit Presets, Fuel, Weight & Balance, Ground Services, Cabin & Doors, and a feature named “Reposition”. The tablet also shows pages for an ACARS Dashboard, Operating FP, Weather & NOTAM, Map, and Performance Calculator. We are also presented with a SimBrief-linked planning page, with OFP import capability, alongside other flight data, route details, and a full load sheet. Furthermore, there is a cabin and doors page, with control over multiple doors, ground service status, and separate lighting sliders for the ceiling, windows, and entry area. The third preview has some weight and balance figures, with a full centre of gravity envelope chart, current gross weight and CG readout. These new previews follow BlueBird’s FSWeekend 2026 appearance in Lelystad, where they reiterated that the 757-200 remains on track for Q3 2026. As of that update, stage 2 beta testing is expected to begin in early April. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Laminar Research Releases X-Plane 12.4.1 and Shares Roadmap
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. Laminar Research has now pushed XP12 12.4.1 out of public beta and into full release for both direct customers and Steam users, while also outlining what is next for the simulator. As a release, 12.4.1 is a refinement update. The default Airbus A330-300 has gotten a fully simulated Ram Air Turbine, improved TCAS audio, and several smaller fixes across systems and displays. Laminar has also added weather radar overlay support to the X1000’s MFD map, provided the aircraft in question is actually fitted with radar. Elsewhere, Laminar says it has improved real-world weather behaviour, reduced some of the more aggressive cumulonimbus generation, added new scenery assets such as cab-over trucks and updated gravel textures, and included native profiles for more recent Honeycomb hardware. The update also adds 310 refreshed Gateway airports. Laminar claims CPU-side changes should bring around a 15 to 20% uplift in typical scenarios, especially around busier airports and more demanding scenes. They also acknowledged that the recent SDK changes have caused some disruption for plugins, but say those steps were necessary, as XP12 continues moving toward a more multi-threaded future. Full Release Notes Moving on, Laminar has also shared a roadmap for the next few releases. XP12 12.4.2 is planned as a minor bug-fix update, followed by 12.4.3, which is being positioned as the simulator’s next named release. That update will focus on VR, including a full rewrite of the VR system, support for Apple Vision Pro via CloudXR, and more changes still to be detailed. After that, 12.4.4 is currently pencilled in as another bug-fix release. Looking further ahead, 12.5.0 is set to become the next major release, and Laminar is calling it the “Flight Model Update”. According to the current roadmap, this release will bring helicopter flight model improvements, gusting wind changes, Dear ImGui support for UI development, and a new enhanced airport. Also quite excitingly, Laminar also says there is broader engine work happening in the background, with the groundwork for next-generation scenery continuing behind the scenes. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: FSDreamTeam Updates GSX for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. FSDreamTeam has released GSX 3.8.7, a large update for GSX, featuring simultaneous jetway/stairs boarding, Aviramp’s ramped staircase system, alongside a long list of changes, fixes, and backend improvements. The new Aviramp stairs are available in GSX Pro, and can be enabled through an airport profile. GSX now also offers a smarter gate selection menu, which hides stands that are too small for the current aircraft by default. This feature can be toggled on and off. As for simultaneous jetway and rear-stairs boarding/deboarding, when enabled in the airport customisation settings, GSX can now split passengers between the front jetway and the rear door. FSDreamTeam has also added a fallback system, so if a jetway fails to dock, boarding will switch to stairs automatically, instead of boarding no passengers at all. Moving on, there are improvements for airport customisation and handler scripts, expanded Python tools, better gate pre-selection options, and improved waypoint editing. The volume sliders now play a sound check when adjusted, making it easier to hear your changes immediately, and the handling for aircraft without GSX profiles has been added. There is also an expansion of the operator database, with several new handling and catering companies from around the world. ‍ Version 3.8.7 – March 31th, 2026 Full Release Notes ‍ GSX Pro can be purchased here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: PMDG Updates 777 Lineup for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. PMDG has recently issued an update to their Boeing 777 Lineup in Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, bringing a wealthy list of small changes and improvements, including a refactored C*U simulation. Build 2.04.138 represents a couple of months' worth of small fixes being implemented by their developers whenever they had free time during the development of the MSFS 2024 version of their 737 Lineup, with things speeding up once that came to a close. ​ The highlight of the update is a refactoring of the C*U fly-by-wire control system simulation, allowing their 777 to fly more like the real counterpart, retaining the normal pitch/speed interface pilots are used to, but also leveraging the fly-by-wire capabilities to enhance speed and pitch stability, making the flight more stable and pleasurable. ​ PMDG also makes sure to reinforce the fact that Boeing’s implementation of the fly-by-wire system is completely different from Airbus’ own approach, designed to function as an “ideal airplane” where their planes feel more like a regular non-FBW airplane, albeit on “steroids”. ​ Furthermore, they have also given attention to the datalink system, displays, certain cabin features, the flight management system, ground operations, hydraulic system, memory management, ground handling physics, pneumatics, radios, numerous other functions, and more. The complete changelog follows below: ‍ Aircraft Specific: The 777 lineup is available on PMDG’s website, with prices ranging from $59.99 to $77, depending on the variant at hand. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍
